Does that work?

it's show.
And Jason.
the same time.
Hey, yeah, I think it's because I tried to, I accidentally joined on another browser while I was trying to get Fathom in, but that worked.
Hello.
Great.
Thanks, everybody, for joining.
Happy Friday.
Okay.
And yeah, let's quick rundown on some updates.

I'll just start from my side because I think it will affect everybody, but the engineering team had me do a bunch of generation this week and record all of my thoughts, and my thoughts were not good or happy with them because it's in a really bad state, really bad state.
So they had me and Matthew both do that, and I think I was shocked at how bad it

It still was, so, but that was well heard, like Daniel yesterday responded after watching all my looms and was like, okay, this is perfect, this is the kind of comprehensive feedback we were looking for, we're going to change a whole bunch of stuff to fix this.

And a lot of it will be actually kind of removing some of the things that they put in place to direct the output, because I think what he communicated to me was that he didn't realize how powerful and good Claude had become since.
So, ironically, a lot of the stuff that they were putting on top of it was, yeah, like Ehtisham said, like, was actually, like, making it worse than just the thing as it operates, right?
And you can go watch the looms, too, if you are curious, but, like, basically, I was just running in parallel the same generation in Atlas and also in Claude Projects, like, fully popular.
related with all of the client artifacts and any other supplemental documents that I thought might be useful.
One in particular, like, we have this client town that's coming on and they're doing tax stuff.
And they were like, we would really like to address the big, beautiful bill, but it's complicated and we don't understand necessarily because it's a moving target.

But like cloud projects would use a combination of like a bunch of best in class documents of like what the current state is and implications from like, certified accountants and then also would go out to the web to find most recent information and came up with stuff that was very like factual accurate, right?
Like once I went through and fact checked it.
So, you know, I articulated all that for them because I think they need sort of the similar thing where it's kind of acting like a closed notebook, but can go out to the web when it wants to as well.
And rather than just right now, kind of like very like narrowly and specifically calling.
And artifacts in and then using the artifact on that one particular task and then sending it away.
Maybe you saw some of the updates from Matthew too.
So those are some of the like more immediate, hopefully, like pain relieving steps that they took, which was, I mean, the big one was the assignment direction was not used.
It was any effect that it had was strictly placebo effect on the user side.
It was just not used.
So that's a huge change.
And then I don't know that they haven't shared any of this either, but I've seen it from Brad, who is, you know, one of the lead engineers over there.
But they have like, they're testing a new interface that is like literally a chat interface.
So it has a box and it's like, hey, what would you like to do today?
And then you kind of like say, I want to write an article about blah, blah, blah.
And then it goes from there, right?
And taps the pipeline.
So lots of big quality of life improvements coming and hopefully some of these will help sooner rather than later.
Because, yeah, it was just immediately apparent once you start getting into it.
It's like one is like doing something with one hand tied behind your back, and the other is like doing it with like a full service team at your disposal, right?
Like it's night and day.
So I think that's good news.
Stay tuned for more out of that.
And I'm going to continue to do some stuff here.
And then if so, if you have anything tricky that you can send it my way and I'll run some experiments as well and use that to further inform them.
I'm talking to this afternoon, but then I'm going out there next week.
So that's another update for me.
They'll be in San Francisco next week working on PT.

So I should be around roughly the same time as I usually am.
Okay.
And then, yeah, other than that, like we went back and forth on how exactly we would be adding these new clients.

I imagine we'll start bringing in CMs to do generation next week, but I'll confirm next week.
And then the other one is Aimbit, and we are ramping, we can ramp fully on them.

then, again, in combination with this thing, because they have really specific ways they want to talk about the product and the specific, technically challenging product.

And yeah, then Jess has three articles, three approved outlines that she's going to work on.

With AIMBIT and Prophecy, the good thing is both parties approved the new topics that we sent them.

I had three of their articles with me that I needed to edit, so I edited two of them yesterday.

that should be.

So, I think there's a lot we can do there with, hopefully, some of the fixes that we've made, particularly around assignment direction.
Now that that will actually be honored, hopefully that can help there significantly.
So, maybe try rerunning with some of that.
Yes, Saskia?
I chatted with Ehtisham yesterday just about all of this, and I also sent, like, a ticket last week about giving, like, the assignment brief quite a detailed outline, and then it broke, whatever.
But yesterday I did actually try and create an article for Prophecy in Atlas, and I used, so I found.
Like, I spoke to you about this, but I find, like, I give, as you said, like, I give CatchyBT everything that Atlas had, and it generated better articles for me.
I've been taking those, and I've been putting them in today.
Yesterday, I put that into the assignment brief, and it actually flowed through all the way to the end.
Like, it kept an outline, and it filled it in.
It's still a bit weird with formatting and the length of the content and stuff, but I'm fixing that.
But it does seem to be getting a little bit better.
So, yeah, I'm having, in summary, having a little bit of success with putting a nice outline with instructions in the assignment brief.
Yeah, it is honoring that.
And then the thing they want to do now, too, because they don't love that the stuff is being generated outside and then brought in for the outline.
But I don't know if they pushed this yet, but they're working on a fix whereby, in the assignment direction, you can use the Ask.ai feature to have it generate an outline based on a prompt.
So you technically can hopefully do that right there, and it will have the same exact.
Output effect as if you were doing it in ChatGPT or COD, right?
I'll definitely see if that comes in on Prophecy.
Yeah, I can't remember if it's actually live.
I don't think it is yet, but as soon as it is, I'll let you know, or they'll put it in Learning Atlas or somewhere else.
Great.
Cool.

Thank you so much for doing all of that, because, Lish, I think I sent you that message about my thoughts on it, and it seems like you've discovered the same thing, and I'm glad that they've got all that feedback.
Well, yeah, it wasn't, it wasn't, like, unavailable information, which is part of the frustration, but, you know, sometimes people just need to be told in the right way.
Yes, exactly.
Yeah, so thank you for doing that.

That was, that also, the length thing was also the big thing that I pointed to them, because it was, I didn't, my testing methodology was, like, I'm not going to do all the things that you ask us to do up front to try to massage this, because that's not a fair comparison.
A real comparison is,
I spend two minutes at the start of the pipeline, and I spend two minutes on the prompts in cloud projects, it's equivalent time in, and then I don't touch anything, and then I get my result, right?
So which one wins in that scenario?
That's a fair comparison.
Not a, like, do I spend an hour and a half trying to set up the workflow at the beginning so that it produces something okay at the end, right?
That's exactly it.
I was spending more time in Atlas trying to get a better result than it was just to go into cloud and give it the same stuff, yeah, essentially.

Yeah, so they, because the one thing, they kept saying, oh, well, you didn't try to affect that, because I said, every time it was three, three to four thousand words, and I'm like, okay, and they're like, you didn't try to adjust the length, and I'm like, yeah, I didn't, I didn't in cloud either, and it gave me 1,200 to 1,500 word articles every single time, so what's going on?
but it was, it's the keyword stuffing, and the, like, the hard-coded, to Edisham's point earlier, like, all the hard-coded stuff that they, in,
This, that it includes, that over-fluffs it now.
So, once they strip out some of that rigging, it'll work better.
Okay, anything else?
We kind of sidetracked there, Ehtisham.
Were you finished your thought on?
So, I just remembered, like, the cloud account links that you sent out in email, that those expired for me.
So, I got two different emails, but when I clicked the link, the link had already expired.
So, please, please send those out again.
Oh?
Yeah.
You mean for the pod B?
Yeah, yeah.
Those ones.
Yeah, it's weird, Darrell.
Like, I keep getting emails to say, like, you need to join this, you need to sign up.
And when I click on it, the link just doesn't work, or it says it's already been used.
So, yeah, it's a bit odd.
Hmm.
Yeah, I was, I just used it yesterday and this morning, and it worked fine.

Anthropic via pod secure link to log into cloud.ai.
Okay.
Okay.
Okay.
Okay.
It got two emails on July 16th, and the links expired.
Secure links.

Okay, maybe this was a product of, like, oh.
Pardon?
I don't have access to the code accounts for the pod yet.
Okay, let me see.
I have access to the group, so I can do member additions.
Yeah, oh, you should have access to it.
I know you should be getting every email.
Is it on this X's Cloud Pro?
No, no, no, it's not.
It's a different one.
So let me, that's what you're missing.
You're missing the actual email and instructions.
I'll get those.
I'll post them and pin them to the channel, because they're buried somewhere in Slack where Yusuf had outlined them for us.
Yes, that is handy.
Okay.
But, yeah, try that again, because maybe it was just some weird coincidence of, like, multiple people logging in at the same time.
Like, I know there's a small chance that that's possible.
just tried it now to get it, and it's just not working.
Hmm.
Okay.
I'm going to take the risk and log out just so that I can see what happens when I try.
But, yeah, if this doesn't work, I'll try to figure out what's going on with Yusuf and the team over there.
Awesome.
Thank you.
Okay.
Anyone else have anything?
No.
I want to say that I tried the AI feature, SKI feature, today on my articles, and it's excellent, really.
It works.
So, please try it on your article as well.
you.

It really is.
It's a...

It can also turn some parts in H3, so for better readability, etc.
So yeah, please read that on your article, Cesar.
That's great.
Yeah, I was using it a bit and it was doing some unusual activity, but it was working a lot better than before.

And even though that's like counterintuitive versus like what a normal manual editing flow would be like, I much prefer it.
But because the other one kind of like, it would highlight in green the changes and then I would approve, but then it left a lot of detritus that was like unintentional in there.
Like,
Some of the old text, some of the new text.

Awesome.
I think that improved.

you.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Weekly pod stand-up to discuss updates, challenges, and improvements in content generation processes.](https://fathom.video/share/YdJp81yCfYsAdegns_3r3XxKEUNx4SsJ?tab=summary&timestamp=1.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Atlas (internal tool) performance issues identified; engineering team implementing fixes based on comprehensive feedback](https://fathom.video/share/YdJp81yCfYsAdegns_3r3XxKEUNx4SsJ?tab=summary&timestamp=34.0)
  - [New clients (Town, Engine, Aimbit) being onboarded with adjusted processes](https://fathom.video/share/YdJp81yCfYsAdegns_3r3XxKEUNx4SsJ?tab=summary&timestamp=291.0)
  - [Cloud Projects outperforming Atlas in content generation; team adapting workflows](https://fathom.video/share/YdJp81yCfYsAdegns_3r3XxKEUNx4SsJ?tab=summary&timestamp=244.0)
  - [Significant improvements to Atlas expected soon, including chat interface and better assignment direction handling](https://fathom.video/share/YdJp81yCfYsAdegns_3r3XxKEUNx4SsJ?tab=summary&timestamp=233.0)

## Topics

### Atlas Performance Issues and Improvements

  - [Darrell provided extensive feedback on Atlas's poor performance compared to Claude Projects](https://fathom.video/share/YdJp81yCfYsAdegns_3r3XxKEUNx4SsJ?tab=summary&timestamp=34.0)
  - [Engineering team acknowledged issues; removing constraining elements to leverage Claude's improved capabilities](https://fathom.video/share/YdJp81yCfYsAdegns_3r3XxKEUNx4SsJ?tab=summary&timestamp=76.0)
  - [New chat interface being tested for more intuitive user experience](https://fathom.video/share/YdJp81yCfYsAdegns_3r3XxKEUNx4SsJ?tab=summary&timestamp=219.0)
  - [Assignment direction feature now functional, honoring user input](https://fathom.video/share/YdJp81yCfYsAdegns_3r3XxKEUNx4SsJ?tab=summary&timestamp=188.0)
  - [Keyword stuffing and hard-coded elements causing over-fluffed content; to be stripped out](https://fathom.video/share/YdJp81yCfYsAdegns_3r3XxKEUNx4SsJ?tab=summary&timestamp=95.0)
  - [Ask.ai feature for outline generation in assignment direction coming soon](https://fathom.video/share/YdJp81yCfYsAdegns_3r3XxKEUNx4SsJ?tab=summary&timestamp=596.0)

### New Client Onboarding

  - [Town and Engine: Pausing at CM layer; Darrell acting as CM temporarily](https://fathom.video/share/YdJp81yCfYsAdegns_3r3XxKEUNx4SsJ?tab=summary&timestamp=300.0)
  - [Aimbit: Full ramp-up approved; demanding but communicative client](https://fathom.video/share/YdJp81yCfYsAdegns_3r3XxKEUNx4SsJ?tab=summary&timestamp=318.0)
  - [Specific product terminology important for Aimbit; team learning to adapt](https://fathom.video/share/YdJp81yCfYsAdegns_3r3XxKEUNx4SsJ?tab=summary&timestamp=332.0)

### Content Generation Workflows

  - [Team experimenting with Cloud Projects for better results](https://fathom.video/share/YdJp81yCfYsAdegns_3r3XxKEUNx4SsJ?tab=summary&timestamp=244.0)
  - [Saskia finding success with detailed outlines in assignment briefs](https://fathom.video/share/YdJp81yCfYsAdegns_3r3XxKEUNx4SsJ?tab=summary&timestamp=532.0)
  - [Joe reported significant improvements in Atlas's AI/SKI feature for editing](https://fathom.video/share/YdJp81yCfYsAdegns_3r3XxKEUNx4SsJ?tab=summary&timestamp=892.0)

### Aftershoot Client Update

  - [New topics and lighter outlines being developed (10 planned for today)](https://fathom.video/share/YdJp81yCfYsAdegns_3r3XxKEUNx4SsJ?tab=summary&timestamp=371.0)
  - [3 approved outlines for Jess to work on](https://fathom.video/share/YdJp81yCfYsAdegns_3r3XxKEUNx4SsJ?tab=summary&timestamp=383.0)
  - [Some articles require editing, one needs complete rewrite](https://fathom.video/share/YdJp81yCfYsAdegns_3r3XxKEUNx4SsJ?tab=summary&timestamp=389.0)

### Prophecy and Aimbit Progress

  - [Both clients approved new topics, leaning towards thought leadership](https://fathom.video/share/YdJp81yCfYsAdegns_3r3XxKEUNx4SsJ?tab=summary&timestamp=400.0)
  - [Saskia to work on Prophecy articles next week](https://fathom.video/share/YdJp81yCfYsAdegns_3r3XxKEUNx4SsJ?tab=summary&timestamp=420.0)
  - [Fey to start on Aimbit articles with approved topics](https://fathom.video/share/YdJp81yCfYsAdegns_3r3XxKEUNx4SsJ?tab=summary&timestamp=420.0)

### Cloud Account Access Issues

  - [Some team members experiencing expired links for Cloud.ai access](https://fathom.video/share/YdJp81yCfYsAdegns_3r3XxKEUNx4SsJ?tab=summary&timestamp=744.0)
  - [Darrell to investigate and provide updated access information](https://fathom.video/share/YdJp81yCfYsAdegns_3r3XxKEUNx4SsJ?tab=summary&timestamp=828.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Darrell to share updated Cloud.ai access information in Slack](https://fathom.video/share/YdJp81yCfYsAdegns_3r3XxKEUNx4SsJ?tab=summary&timestamp=828.0)
  - [Team to continue testing and providing feedback on Atlas improvements](https://fathom.video/share/YdJp81yCfYsAdegns_3r3XxKEUNx4SsJ?tab=summary&timestamp=1054.0)
  - [Ehtisham to deliver new topics and outlines for Aftershoot](https://fathom.video/share/YdJp81yCfYsAdegns_3r3XxKEUNx4SsJ?tab=summary&timestamp=371.0)
  - [Saskia to begin work on Prophecy articles next week](https://fathom.video/share/YdJp81yCfYsAdegns_3r3XxKEUNx4SsJ?tab=summary&timestamp=420.0)
  - [Fey to start on Aimbit articles](https://fathom.video/share/YdJp81yCfYsAdegns_3r3XxKEUNx4SsJ?tab=summary&timestamp=420.0)
  - [Team to experiment with improved AI/SKI feature in Atlas for editing](https://fathom.video/share/YdJp81yCfYsAdegns_3r3XxKEUNx4SsJ?tab=summary&timestamp=892.0)

action_items.description: Run experiments w/ tricky content in Atlas & Cloud Projects. Send results to eng team.
Create 10 new topics & lighter outlines for Aftershoot. Send to client.
Edit 2 Aftershoot articles. Rewrite 1 Aftershoot article w/ new structure.
Review AIMBIT acct status. Check article count, delivery rate, etc.
Edit 3rd AIMBIT article. Send msg to client re: edits.
Post & pin Cloud.ai login instructions in team channel.
